title: How To Get All-On-4 Dental Implants Step By Step?
article: The all-on-4 dental implant treatment is very popular because it works well and is stable. Many people choose this option. However, some of you may feel nervous before getting the treatment. To help with any worry, we will explain all the steps of the treatment process, which might help you relax.

title: How To Get All-On-4 Dental Implants Step By Step?
article:
Step 1: Consultation And Pre-Op Assessment

Before undergoing the All-on-4 dental implants procedure, you are first consulted. During the consultation, your dental health and medical history are reviewed. Any underlying issues are also addressed. Once you understand the procedure and are deemed suitable, the dentist proceeds to the next step.

title: How To Get All-On-4 Dental Implants Step By Step?
article:
Step 2: Pre-Treatment Procedures

In this step, the dentist measures the inside of your mouth and plans for accurate implant placement. This is crucial for a perfect fit, comfort, and stability. Any necessary pretreatment procedures will be done in advance. These may include tooth extractions or bone grafting surgeries, ensuring optimal oral condition for the next step.

title: How To Get All-On-4 Dental Implants Step By Step?
article:
Step 3: Implant Placement

First, anesthesia is given to make you comfortable during the surgery. Then, the All-on-4 method is used to put four titanium screws in each part of your mouth. These screws act as a strong foundation for the fake teeth. It takes about 3-6 months for the screws to fully join with the jawbone. During this time, temporary dentures are worn until the screws are ready to support permanent replacements.

title: How To Get All-On-4 Dental Implants Step By Step?
article:
Step 4: Implant Abutment Placement

Once the implants have fused with your jawbone, tiny parts called abutments are connected to securely hold the fake teeth. Then, the dentist creates molds of your mouth and sends them to a specialized dental lab. They use these molds to make a personalized set of artificial teeth. These teeth can either be removed or permanently attached in the mouth, depending on your preference and the dentist's recommendation.

title: How To Get All-On-4 Dental Implants Step By Step?
article:
Step 5: Prosthesis Placement

After the dental lab finishes making the special prosthetic, it is firmly connected to the abutments on the implants. The dentist adjusts it if necessary to ensure it fits well and works correctly. The All-on-4 procedure is not considered complete until this is achieved.
